modules.module_title: NATP Module 13: Long Term Care Resident
modules.source_status_flag: Generated from approved Module 13 PDF source map.
learning_objectives: Describe common basic human needs and interventions for the elderly patient/resident; environmental, psychological, social, recreational and spiritual
Describe common community resources to meet the needs of the elderly
Describe the special needs of persons with developmental and mental disorders including intellectual disability, cerebral palsy, epilepsy, Parkinson’s disease, and mental illness
Describe the special needs of persons with Alzheimer’s Disease and other related dementias
Describe the body’s basic organization and composition

List the body systems, including basic anatomy and physiology, common diseases of the elderly with signs and symptoms, Nurse Assistant duties and observations, aging changes and complications of immobility
Describe changes in body systems associated with aging

cards.narration_script:
Source-traced teaching outline: Review, spell, pronounce, and use Module 13 terminology in proper context. Terminology covers long-term care resident vocabulary, medical/anatomical terms, body-system terms, conditions, signs/symptoms, and care-related vocabulary. Use terms appropriately when charting and reporting to licensed personnel.

CNA boundary: follow the care plan and facility policy, observe, document as assigned, and report unusual signs/symptoms or unsafe changes to licensed staff. This dry run does not validate hands-on competency or claim clinical-hour credit.

internal_challenge.prompt: For Module 13 Objective 1, which response best preserves CNA scope and the source-first boundary?

choices.label: Independently diagnose, prescribe, or change care outside CNA scope.
Follow the care plan/source guidance, observe/document as assigned, and report unusual signs or unsafe changes to licensed staff.
Use prior Module 10/11 generated content as the source authority when Module 13 is incomplete.
Use online dry-run completion as proof of hands-on competency or clinical-hour credit.

internal_challenge.rationale_internal: The source keeps CNA work within care-plan follow-through, observation, documentation where assigned, and reporting to licensed staff; the dry run does not validate competency or claim clinical credit.
